records.ety: [L. consistens, p. pr.: cf. F. consistant.]

defs: Possessing firmness or fixedness; firm; hard; solid.
quotes: The humoral and consistent parts of the body.

defs: Having agreement with itself or with something else; having harmony among its parts; possessing unity; accordant; harmonious; congruous; compatible; uniform; not contradictory.
quotes: Show me one that has it in his power To act consistent with himself an hour.
With reference to such a lord, to serve and to be free are terms not consistent only, but equivalent.

defs: Living or acting in conformity with one's belief or professions.
quotes: It was utterly to be at once a consistent Quaker and a conspirator.
